statusLine: EU fuel security is under strain with 19 of 27 countries holding critical-level reserves in at least one fuel type, while oil prices have surged 20.5% since February geopolitical tensions.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security faces significant pressure as widespread reserve depletion affects the majority of EU member states. Of the 27 countries analyzed, 19 hold critical-level reserves in at least one fuel category, with several major economies including France, Germany, Italy, and Spain experiencing severe shortfalls. Diesel reserves are particularly concerning, with 15 countries below the 90-day mandatory minimum, averaging just 88.8 days EU-wide.

The crisis is most acute in Central and Eastern Europe, where Luxembourg, Poland, Austria, and Romania show critical levels across all three fuel types. Even traditionally well-stocked Nordic countries show vulnerabilities, with jet fuel reserves critically low in Finland (31.9 days) and Denmark (75.7 days). This widespread depletion likely reflects recent strategic reserve releases following Middle East supply disruptions.

Oil price pressures compound these supply concerns, with Brent crude climbing to $87.33 per barrel—a 20.5% increase since late February tensions escalated. This translates to elevated fuel costs across the bloc, with pump prices ranging from €1.34 per liter in Malta to €2.39 in Denmark for petrol. The combination of depleted reserves and rising costs creates a challenging environment for European energy security, though some countries like Estonia, Ireland, and the Netherlands maintain stronger buffer positions.
keyPoints: 19 of 27 EU countries hold critical reserves in at least one fuel type, with diesel most affected (15 countries below 90-day minimum)
Major economies France, Germany, Italy and Spain all show critical shortfalls, indicating widespread rather than localized pressure
Oil prices have surged 20.5% since February geopolitical escalation, from $72.48 to $87.33 per barrel
Central and Eastern European countries face the most severe shortages, with Luxembourg, Poland, Austria and Romania critical across all fuel types
Jet fuel reserves show particular vulnerability with 18 countries below minimum levels, averaging just 87.3 days EU-wide
